expressions.english: smiling face
Don't laugh.
I'm sorry for laughing.
expressions.notes: 미안하다 = to be sorry; to feel bad

expressions.english: A smile will bring luck.
Show me a big smile.
expressions.notes:
환하다 = to be bright
환하게 is an adverb form of 환하다.
-이/어/아 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.english: It's not something you can laugh about. It's no laughing matter.
expressions.notes: 웃을 일 = something to laugh about, laughable matter

expressions.english: Thanks for making me smile.
expressions.notes:
-이/어/아 주다 = to do something for someone
고마다 = to be grateful

expressions.english: Smiling is good for your health.
expressions.notes:
건강 = one's health
-에 좋다 = to be good for + N

expressions.english: Lately, there's nothing to laugh about.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
없다 = to not have; to not be there; to not exist

expressions.english: Promise me you won't laugh.
expressions.notes: 약속하다 = to promise, make a promise

expressions.english: You look good with a smile on your face.
expressions.notes:
-기 좋다 = to be good for + V-ing
보기 좋다 = to look good to see

expressions.english: Stop laughing and start talking.
People laughed at me.
expressions.notes: -들 is a suffix used to indicate plural.

expressions.english: I laughed so hard that my stomach hurts.
expressions.notes:
배 = stomach, belly
아프다 = to be sick, hurt

expressions.korean: 제 이야기를 듣고 웃으면 안 돼요.
expressions.english: You shouldn't laugh at my story.
expressions.notes:
이야기 = story; what one says
듣다 = to hear; to listen
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.english: Without even meaning to, I laughed out loud.
expressions.notes:
모르게 = without knowing; without meaning to + V
뛰다 = to chat; to make noise
사이 = while + S +V; between; among
되다 = to become

expressions.english: While we were laughing and talking, it had already become nighttime.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 (으)ㄹ수록 = the more ..., the more ...
-이/어/아지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.english: The more you laugh, the better you will feel.
expressions.notes:
스트레스 = stress
스트레스가 풀리다 = stress is relieved

expressions.english: I laughed a lot, and it helped me to relieve some stress.
expressions.notes:
한번 = once, one time
-기 시작하다 = to start + V-ing
멈추다 = to stop
-(으)ㄹ 수 없다 = to be not able to + V, can't

expressions.korean: 한번 웃기 시작하니까 멈출 수가 없었어요.
expressions.english: Once I started laughing, I couldn't stop it.
expressions.notes:
한번 = once, one time
-기 시작하다 = to start + V-ing
멈추다 = to stop
-(으)ㄹ 수 없다 = to be not able to + V, can't
